key_learnings: Dietary patterns trump single nutrients for cardiovascular protection — the Mediterranean and DASH diets reduce CVD risk by 25–30%. Saturated fat replacement with unsaturated fats (especially polyunsaturated) reduces LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular events. Dietary sodium restriction (<2.3 g/day) is effective for blood pressure management, particularly in salt-sensitive individuals.
research_findings: Every 1% of energy from saturated fat replaced by polyunsaturated fat reduces coronary heart disease risk by ~2–3%. Plant sterols (2g/day) reduce LDL cholesterol by 5–10% without medications. The PREDIMED study showed olive oil-enriched Mediterranean diet reduced major cardiovascular events by 30% vs. control.
current_focus_areas: Developing lipid-lowering dietary protocols beyond statin therapy; building sodium tracking tools for hypertension management; researching the role of polyphenols, flavonoids, and nitrate-rich vegetables in vascular health.
